response: The EU's General Data Protection Regulation, which took effect in May 2018, fundamentally changed data privacy by applying to any company processing EU residents' data, requiring provisions like the right to erasure and 72-hour breach reporting, and imposing fines of up to 4% of global revenue or €20 million. Major enforcement actions include massive fines against tech giants such as Meta (€1.2 billion), Amazon (€746 million), and Google (over €200 million). While the regulation has inspired similar laws globally—including in California, Brazil, and India—critics argue it creates heavy compliance burdens and inconsistent enforcement. Despite these challenges, GDPR established data privacy as a fundamental right and set the worldwide standard for data protection.
